statement.p: Kara and Lindsay are working together to paint a room. Kara works 3.5 times as fast as Lindsay does. If they work together, it took them 7 hours to complete the job. Answer the following questions:
If Kara paints the room alone, it would take her hours to complete the job.
If Lindsay paints the room alone, it would take her hours to complete the job.

solution.p: Lets understand how to use fractions to model work problems. Assume it takes a person 5 hours to complete a job. If we treat the whole job as one whole, each hour, this person would complete \frac{1}{5} of the job.
Similarly, if it takes a person x hours to complete a job, each hour, this person would complete \frac{1}{x} of the job.
Now we are ready to model this situation with math.
Assume that, if Kara paints the room alone, it would take her x hours to complete the job. Each hour, Kara does \frac{1}{x} of the whole job.
Since Kara works 3.5 times as fast as Lindsay does, if Lindsay paints the room alone, it would take her 3.5x hours to complete the job. Each hour, Lindsay does \frac{1}{3.5x} of the whole job.
Its given that, if they work together, it took them 7 hours to complete the job. Each hour, they can complete \frac{1}{7} of the whole job.
Now we can write an equation and solve it:
In the second step, to get rid of all fractions, we multiplied each term with the common denominator {3.5\cdot 7x}.
By the solution we got, it would take Kara {9} hours to complete the job.
Since Kara works 3.5 times as fast as Lindsay does, it would take Lindsay 3.5\cdot{9}={31.5} hours to complete the job.
